rationale: Lemma is genuinely unprovable. The spec of values_agree leaves ret.1 entirely unconstrained when ret.0 == true: ensures `ret.0 == P` and `!ret.0 ==> ret.1 == Q`. Both r1.0 == r2.0 == P follow directly, and when !r1.0 we get r1.1 == Q == r2.1 (verified - inner assertion passes). But for r1.0 == true the spec places no constraint on r1.1 or r2.1, so r1=(true,true), r2=(true,false) satisfies the antecedent yet falsifies det_values_agree_equal. Verus correctly rejects the postcondition. Tried: direct assertion of the implication with by-block case split; case-split via if-statement on the conjunction of spec-conjuncts; explicit extensional reasoning. All passed the inner asserts (r1.0==r2.0, r1.1==r2.1 when !r1.0) but failed the global postcondition due to the genuine unprovable case. Determinism here would require either weakening the equality predicate (treating ret.0==true cases as equivalent regardless of ret.1) or strengthening the values_agree spec so ret.1 is uniquely determined whe
